description:
You are a FinOps analyst who has just received two comma-separated reports and you want to line-up their key columns side-by-side so you can start calculating costs.  
    There are already three directories in your home directory:  

      • /home/user/finops/raw      ← contains the two source *.csv files  
      • /home/user/finops/work     ← scratch space for any temporary files you need  
      • /home/user/finops/output   ← where all final artefacts **must** be written  

    Your job is to use classic “column cutting” and “column pasting” commands (cut, paste and only very light shell plumbing) to build a clean, tab-separated file that contains one line per cloud service.

    1. Source material (already present):  
       a) /home/user/finops/raw/usage_report.csv  
          Columns in order: Service,ResourceID,HoursUsed,Region (there is a header line).  
       b) /home/user/finops/raw/pricing.csv  
          Columns in order: Service,PricePerHourUSD,Region,LastUpdated (there is a header line).

    2. Desired final data file (you must create it):  
       /home/user/finops/output/aggregated_service_costs.tsv

       Exact format requirements that the automated tests will check:  
         • It is **tab-separated**, *not* comma-separated.  
         • It has exactly 4 lines: one header + three data lines.  
         • Header (line-1) text must be **exactly** – including case and spacing:  
                Service<TAB>HoursUsed<TAB>PricePerHourUSD  
         • Lines 2-4 must contain the following fields in this order, separated by a single TAB and terminated by a single newline:  
              1) EC2    720   0.023  
              2) S3     0     0.000  
              3) RDS    300   0.250  

       The tests will parse the file strictly; any extra spaces, extra tabs, blank lines or CR characters will cause failure.

    3. Required verification log (you must create it):  
       /home/user/finops/output/aggregation_log.txt

       Format (exactly two lines, each terminated by a single newline):  
         LINE-1  COUNT=3  
         LINE-2  MD5=<32-character-hexadecimal-checksum>  

       • The value after “COUNT=” must be the integer count of *data* lines in aggregated_service_costs.tsv (i.e. 3).  
       • The value after “MD5=” must be the lowercase MD5 checksum of the file /home/user/finops/output/aggregated_service_costs.tsv itself.  
       • There must be **no** extra spaces before or after the ‘=’.  
       • The automated grader will compute its own MD5 of the TSV and compare it to what you wrote, so be sure to calculate it correctly inside the shell.

    4. Operational constraints:  
       • You are limited to standard GNU/Linux CLI utilities that should already exist in a minimal distribution (cut, paste, md5sum, sort, etc.).  
       • Do *not* modify the original CSV files.  
       • Intermediate scratch files, if you want them, must go in /home/user/finops/work; the grader will ignore that directory.  
       • You do **not** need to aggregate, sum or otherwise transform numeric values—just align the columns exactly as described.  
       • Solve the problem entirely from the command line; do not open an interactive editor.

    5. Success criteria:  
       • /home/user/finops/output/aggregated_service_costs.tsv exists, follows the exact 4-line, tab-separated format above, and is newline-terminated at the end of the last line.  
       • /home/user/finops/output/aggregation_log.txt exists and its COUNT value is 3 and its MD5 value is correct for the TSV file.  
       • You achieved this primarily with ‘cut’ and ‘paste’ commands (other utils for header insertion, checksum calculation, etc. are fine).

    When you’re done, you can display the two files to the console so a human can visually confirm them, but make sure they physically exist on disk exactly as specified.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing source files and their contents -->
    <file path="/home/user/finops/raw/usage_report.csv"><![CDATA[Service,ResourceID,HoursUsed,Region
EC2,i-123456,720,us-east-1
S3,bucket-abc,0,us-east-1
RDS,db-654321,300,us-west-2
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/finops/raw/pricing.csv"><![CDATA[Service,PricePerHourUSD,Region,LastUpdated
EC2,0.023,us-east-1,2023-09-01
S3,0.000,us-east-1,2023-09-01
RDS,0.250,us-west-2,2023-09-01
]]></file>

    <!-- Expected final artefact after correct completion -->
    <file path="/home/user/finops/output/aggregated_service_costs.tsv" should_exist="yes"><![CDATA[Service	HoursUsed	PricePerHourUSD
EC2	720	0.023
S3	0	0.000
RDS	300	0.250
]]></file>

    <!-- The grader will compute this checksum at runtime, but we note the value here for reference -->
    <md5_of_aggregated_service_costs_tsv>c0a2e81e2d6a64481ef42938ef30b650</md5_of_aggregated_service_costs_tsv>

    <!-- Expected log structure (the actual MD5 in the second line must match the file built by the agent) -->
    <file path="/home/user/finops/output/aggregation_log.txt" should_exist="yes">
        Line-1 must be exactly:  COUNT=3
        Line-2 must be exactly:  MD5=<32-character-hex-string matching the real checksum of aggregated_service_costs.tsv>
    </file>
name: task_000000_b473365e
